resources.string: A simple, beautiful way to give at the masjid
Setup in progress — this kiosk is not ready to take donations yet.
Enter your masjid server address and the 6-digit code shown in Admin → Devices.
Enter the full https:// address shown in Admin → Devices.
That code didn\'t work. Generate a fresh code in Admin → Devices and try again.
Couldn\'t reach the server. Check the address, and that this tablet and the server are on the same Wi-Fi.
Couldn\'t secure the connection to the server. Ask an admin to check the setup.
Something went wrong. Please try again.
The server\'s security certificate changed, so this kiosk locked itself for safety. Pair it again to reconnect.
Ask a masjid admin if you don\'t have it.
That PIN wasn\'t right. Try again.
Too many attempts. Try again in %1$d seconds.
No exit PIN is set yet. Set one in Admin → Devices to protect this kiosk.

A USB card reader connects on its own — just plug it in. Use the button below only for a Bluetooth reader. No reader at all? Turn on “Allow manual card entry” in Admin → Giving screen and donors can type their card.
Updating the reader — keep it powered and nearby.
Finish Payments setup (Stripe account + a card-reader location) in the admin panel before connecting a reader.
Bluetooth and Location permission are needed to find a reader. Enable them for this app in the tablet\'s Settings, then try again.
Version %1$s is available (this kiosk is on %2$s).

Tapping Update app opens the newest version in this tablet\'s browser to download and install — the same way the app was first installed. You may need to allow your browser to install apps and tap through a security notice for your server.
To leave kiosk mode, set an exit PIN in Admin → Devices, then unlock with it. This keeps the kiosk locked until the PIN reaches this tablet.
Keeps the notification shade closed while the kiosk is locked.

Optional. While the kiosk is locked, this closes the notification shade / quick-settings the moment it is pulled down, so a donor can\'t reach settings from it. It reads no screen content and does nothing when the kiosk is unlocked (maintenance) or unpaired. Turn it on for the strongest lock without a computer.

Lock this tablet down — no computer needed. On this tablet open Settings → Security and: 1) turn ON \"Screen pinning\" (some tablets call it \"App pinning\"), 2) turn ON \"Ask for PIN/pattern before unpinning\", and 3) set a screen lock (PIN or pattern). The kiosk then pins itself: the notification shade and the Home/Recents buttons are blocked, and leaving needs your device PIN. Also tap \"Set as Home app\" below so Home returns here, and \"Turn on shade lock\" to enable the accessibility helper that keeps the notification shade shut. (An even stronger, fully un-leavable lock is available with one-time device-owner setup — see docs/TABLET_SETUP.md — but it\'s optional.)
